questions.question: REPLICA IDENTITY DEFAULT vs FULL для CDC tables — какой использовать и почему?

options.text: DEFAULT (uses PK) — стандарт для most tables. FULL шлёт all old columns в UPDATE/DELETE event — нужно если downstream требует before-values (e.g. status transition tracking, complex enrichment). Trade-off: FULL имеет performance impact на Postgres writes — increased WAL volume + network traffic

questions.explanation: Critical Postgres CDC choice. DEFAULT (using PK): только key columns в UPDATE/DELETE before-image, minimal overhead, OK для most analytics. FULL: complete old row before UPDATE/DELETE, needed для downstream materialized views with aggregations или change-pattern detection. Performance cost FULL: каждый write logs full old row в WAL = 2-5x increased WAL volume + network bandwidth + storage. Critical OLTP tables — measure impact в staging. Capstone: orders use FULL (для status tracking), customers use DEFAULT (simpler).

questions.question: Initial snapshot phase в production — какие practices обязательны для избежания production impact?

options.text: (1) Read replica для source, не primary; (2) Запуск ночью / low-traffic window; (3) scan.incremental.snapshot.enabled=true (lock-free); (4) Initial parallelism меньше peak (4 vs 8) во время snapshot; (5) Chunk size 100k для balance memory/throughput; (6) Monitor Postgres pg_stat_activity COPY queries; (7) Verify replica lag < 1min до запуска; (8) Pre-snapshot validation queries
Запустить в peak hours для thorough load testing

questions.explanation: Snapshot phase critical для production safety. Parallel SELECT * scans on 100M+ tables load Postgres heavily. Practices: (1) read replica для load isolation; (2) off-peak timing reduces user impact; (3) incremental snapshot is lock-free + parallelizable; (4) reduced parallelism during snapshot, scale up after; (5) chunk size balances memory + throughput; (6) monitor Postgres-side query activity; (7) verify replica health pre-snapshot. Без этих practices: production app latency spikes, possible downtime. Treat snapshot как scheduled maintenance event.
questions.studyHint: Повторите урок 03 — раздел 'Step 4: Initial snapshot strategy'

questions.question: Replication slot lifecycle — что произойдёт если Flink CDC job permanently stopped без cleanup?

options.text: Slot persistently держит WAL retention reference. Postgres не truncate WAL до confirmed_flush_lsn от slot. WAL накапливается = диск переполняется → Postgres crash. Mitigation: monitor slot lag, max_slot_wal_keep_size как safety bound (Postgres dropped slot если exceeded), explicit pg_drop_replication_slot для retired pipelines

questions.explanation: Replication slot — persistent reservation. Не automatically cleaned. Если pipeline permanently retired без `SELECT pg_drop_replication_slot('flink_cdc')`, slot continues holding WAL retention. На high-traffic OLTP: GB/час accumulation. Real incidents: forgotten test slots filling production Postgres disk over weeks. Production guards: (1) max_slot_wal_keep_size automatic drop if exceeded; (2) alerting на slot lag > X GB; (3) retirement runbook включает explicit slot drop; (4) periodic audit pg_replication_slots для orphan slots.

questions.question: Schema evolution с `schema.change.behavior: try-evolve` гарантирует zero downtime для всех schema changes в production.
questions.explanation: try-evolve attempts apply but logs warning if не can. Не gives zero downtime guarantee. (1) Destructive changes (DROP COLUMN, RENAME) — try-evolve может gracefully skip, но downstream queries относящие к removed column сломаются (это data issue, не Flink issue); (2) Incompatible type changes (VARCHAR → INTEGER) — both modes fail. Безopasность: production prod должен иметь Postgres DDL governance + alerting на destructive changes + lakehouse retention 30+ days для recovery. try-evolve вспомогательный tool, не silver bullet.
